title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are searching for a suspect in a Jan. 22 armed robbery in Dorchester.
The suspect reportedly robbed multiple victims at gunpoint near Adams Street and Whitten Street around 4:30 p.m.
Police request public assistance and offer anonymous tips through 1-800-494-TIPS or by texting 'TIP' to CRIME (27463).

title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials threaten to withhold license for World Cup unless public safety funding is provided upfront by March 17.
Boston FIFA World Cup 2026 host committee claims to have funds and Kraft backing but will not pay safety costs upfront.
Massachusetts' share of $625 million federal security funding delayed; state confident games will proceed as scheduled.

title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu will host her first 'office hours' for Boston residents to express concerns directly to her.
The event will take place Thursday at the Bruce C. Bolling Building in Roxbury with city staff present.
City services like voting registration and certificate printing will be available during the two-hour event.

title: 7 players Bruins could target at 2026 NHL trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are exploring options to strengthen their roster before the 2026 NHL trade deadline.
Potential trade targets include Robert Thomas, Conor Garland, Rasmus Ristolainen, Justin Faulk, Simon Nemec, Zach Whitecloud, and Owen Tippett.
The Bruins aim to acquire players with long-term contracts, avoiding rentals in search of sustainable roster improvements.

title: Staff member at special needs residential program in Franklin accused of breaking autistic student’s arm
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Saturs Allison, 27, charged with assault for allegedly breaking an autistic student's arm at Amego in Franklin.
Incident occurred on Oct. 13, 2025, reportedly captured on security footage showing the use of excessive force.
Allison was arraigned in Norfolk Superior Court and faces up to five years in prison if convicted.

title: ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Amanda Lewis, 18, from Charlotte, NC, is running the Boston Marathon for her late friend Lily.
Lewis is participating with Dana-Farber, inspired by her father's involvement in the Marathon Challenge.
Her run is dedicated to raising funds for cancer research and honoring friends affected by cancer.

title: Fall River man sentenced for raping woman when she refused to take naked pictures of young daughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Elias Rodrigues Dos Santos Mendes pleaded guilty to rape and child pornography charges in Fall River Superior Court.
He was sentenced to eight to ten years in state prison and will be deported to Brazil after serving his term.
Mendes was convicted of raping a woman who refused to take nude photos of her daughter for him.

title: What’s next for Patriots’ offense after releasing Stefon Diggs?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots plan to release receiver Stefon Diggs, freeing up $17 million in cap space.
Potential targets to replace Diggs include free agents Alec Pierce and A.J. Brown.
Patriots hold the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft for potential wide receiver additions.

title: Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brianna Poehler of Granby, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with the Miles for Miracles team.
Poehler's daughter survived a liver transplant at Boston Children's Hospital at 11 months old, inspiring her marathon effort.
Poehler aims to raise funds for Boston Children's Hospital through her marathon run, honoring her daughter's survival.

title: Do you support the U.S. strikes on Iran? Here’s what 300 readers said.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: 54% of over 350 respondents support U.S. strikes on Iran, while 43% oppose, and 3% are unsure.
Supporters cite Iran's role in regional conflicts and view the regime as a threat to international stability.
Opponents fear the strikes could lead to a broader war and criticize the lack of congressional approval.
